concept_name: Resistivity — Shape Times Material (R = ρL/A, ρ = m/ne²τ, ρ_T = ρ₀(1+αΔT))
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.3 Current Electricity (§3.6 Resistivity of Various Materials, §3.7 Temperature Dependence of Resistance) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. DC Pandey consulted for chapter SCOPE only (Current Electricity ToC: resistivity / temperature-dependence / combination-of-resistors sections) to confirm atomic boundaries — no teaching method, example problem, or figure imported. HC Verma consulted for teaching SEQUENCE (stretch the wire longer and watch current fall — geometry alone can change resistance; widen it and watch current climb back; cycle geometry through three phases including the constant-volume stretch trap while pinning resistivity beside the dancing resistance readout as the PRIMARY aha; swap the material itself to show resistivity is what the material IS, not its shape; heat the metal to show the microscopic mechanism behind temperature dependence; contrast with a near-zero-alpha alloy; hand the explorer all four dials), never copied. The atomic idea: resistance factorizes into shape × material — R = ρL/A, where resistivity ρ = m/(ne²τ) is the material's own number (set by n and τ, independent of geometry) and, for metals, rises with temperature as ρ_T = ρ₀(1 + α(T − T₀)) because hotter lattices cut τ. SCOPE: this is the THIRD concept of Ch.3, continuous with drift_velocity (SHIPPED, opener) and ohms_law (SHIPPED, second) — same particle_field (2D p5.js) renderer family, same i = neAv_d microscopic chain, this time with L/A/material/T as the driven sliders. Does NOT cover conductivity/current density σ, j (deferred to current_density_conductivity), semiconductor band theory (deferred to Ch.14 semiconductors_energy_bands), superconductivity (deferred to superconductivity), mobility μ = v_d/E (deferred to electron_mobility), series/parallel combinations, or power/heating P = I²R (deferred to electric_power_heating). Renderer-reality note: this concept does NOT drive i/R/rho via physics_engine_config.formulas/PM_interpolate — particle_field_renderer.ts computes them natively in engine JS via the shipped resistivity adapter (condLength/condAreaM2/materialList/materialAt/rhoOf/tauFromRho/curL/curA/curMaterialIndex/curTemperature/curRho/realCurrent/realResistance), reading slider_controls.{L,A,material,T} and formula_anchor.constants.{e,m_e,n,T0_ref,V_supply} — physics_engine_config below is authored as documentation per the Zod requirement. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the three genuine misconceptions — 'resistance is fixed by the material; length and thickness don't matter', 'R is a property of copper itself' (conflating R with ρ), and 'metals conduct better when hot' — are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + straightforward contrast-in-motion beats at STATE_1/STATE_3/STATE_5, no predict-pause). Calibration note: real copper at 1 m/1 mm² gives R = 0.017 Ω, which cannot honestly drive a demo meter off a visible supply — a fixed hidden V_supply (0.0204 V, physConst, never a slider, never shown on screen) is the declared demo adapter (same fudge pattern as ohms_law's τ-adapter) so defaults land i ≈ 1.2 A, continuous with the siblings; n/e/m_e algebraically cancel in the closed form i = V_supply·A/(ρ·L), so R and ρ RATIOS on screen are numerically true-valued independent of that adapter choice — verified via node arithmetic this session (see constraints).

real_world_anchor.primary: Open the back of a room heater — the coil that glows orange is nichrome, but the cord feeding it is copper, and the same current flows through both. The coil is long, thin, and made of a material whose resistivity ρ is about sixty-five times copper's, so almost all the resistance — and therefore almost all the heat — lives in the coil, while the copper cord stays cool in your hand. R = ρL/A, engineered on purpose: the coil maximizes all three levers, the cord minimizes them.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Touch a multimeter to a cold filament bulb and you read a fraction of its rated resistance — the tungsten's ρ climbs several-fold as it heats to glowing; and the reason lab-standard resistance boxes are wound with manganin is exactly its near-zero α — a resistance that refuses to drift with the weather.

L.name: Conductor length
L.role: slider — live from STATE_1; drives R directly (R ∝ L)

A.name: Conductor cross-section area
A.role: slider — live from STATE_2; drives R inversely (R ∝ 1/A)

material.name: Material index (0=copper, 1=nichrome, 2=manganin)
material.role: slider (discrete preset) — live from STATE_4; selects {rho0, alpha, tint} from the materials array

T.name: Temperature
T.role: slider — live from STATE_5; drives rho via rho_T = rho0(1+alpha*(T-T0_ref))

R.name: Resistance
R.role: live readout — computed natively in particle_field_renderer.ts, not via physics_engine_config.formulas

rho.name: Resistivity
rho.role: live readout — computed natively, the material's own fixed number, independent of L and A

i.name: Current
i.role: live readout — computed natively via the current meter

e.name: Elementary charge
e.role: locked constant, shared with drift_velocity/ohms_law — algebraically cancels, not a calibration lever

n.name: Free-electron number density
n.role: locked constant, held fixed across ALL three materials as a declared teaching simplification — algebraically cancels

T0_ref.name: Reference temperature for alpha
T0_ref.role: locked, matches the T slider's own default so rho = rho0 exactly at default

V_supply.name: Fixed hidden internal supply
V_supply.role: locked, NEVER shown to the student — the sole calibration lever; ratios on screen are true-valued independent of its value

formulas.macroscopic_law: R = rho*L/A — the geometric factorization (documentary; the engine computes i from the microscopic bridge below, and R = rho*L/A falls out as a consequence)
formulas.temperature_law: rho_T = rho0*(1 + alpha*(T - T0_ref)) — linear approximation, valid 20-220 C for these three metals/alloys
formulas.microscopic_bridge: rho(T) = rho0(material)*(1+alpha(material)*(T-T0_ref)); tau = m_e/(n*e^2*rho); E = V_supply/L; v_d = e*E*tau/m_e; i = n*e*A*v_d; R = V_supply/i — algebraically equals rho*L/A exactly, for ANY choice of n/e/m_e (they cancel)
formulas.constant_volume_stretch: at constant volume (L*A held fixed), A = A0*L0/L, so R = rho*L^2/(A0*L0) — R scales as L^2, not L; doubling L at constant volume QUADRUPLES R, the stock JEE trap drawn live in STATE_3 phase 3

constraints: R = rho * L / A exactly, at every (L, A, material, T) combination — the geometric factorization is exact, not approximate
rho depends ONLY on material and temperature, never on L or A — geometry and material identity are independent factors
at constant volume (L*A held fixed), R scales as L^2, not L — a stretched-and-thinned wire's resistance quadruples when length doubles
rho_T = rho0 * (1 + alpha*(T - T0_ref)) is a linear approximation valid over 20-220 C for these three metals/alloys; no phase transitions or superconducting/semiconducting regimes are modeled
n (free-electron density) is held fixed across ALL three materials in this simulation as a declared teaching simplification — the taught quantity is rho itself, never n or tau individually

V_supply is a fixed hidden internal constant (0.0204 V), never shown to the student — the taught quantities are R, rho, and their RATIOS across states, which are numerically honest independent of V_supply's value

verified via node arithmetic this session: default L=1,A=1,copper,T=20 -> R=0.0170 Ohm, i=1.2000 A; S1 end L=2 -> i=0.6000 A (exact half); S2 end A=2 -> i=2.4000 A (exact double); S3 constant-volume L=2,A=0.5 -> R=0.0680 Ohm (exactly 4x, confirms quadruples not doubles); S4 copper->nichrome rho ratio = 64.706x; S5 copper T:20->220 rho ratio = 1.780x (i falls to 0.5618x). (STATE_6 manganin-contrast removed by founder 2026-07-08; manganin still reachable in the STATE_7 explore material slider, rho ratio ~1.002x)

aha_moment.statement: Geometry dances, but resistivity never moves — R = ρL/A.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The R readout swings through the whole three-phase geometry cycle while the ρ readout sits pinned at 1.700×10⁻⁸ Ω·m the entire time.

STATE_1.title: Longer chokes: same copper, same battery — stretch it and the current falls

scene_composition.text: Same copper wire, same battery — watch what happens when it simply gets longer.
The wire visibly elongates from one metre to two metres.
The current meter falls — all the way to half.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same copper wire, same battery — watch what happens when it simply gets longer.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही copper wire, वही battery — जब यह सिर्फ लंबा हो जाता है तो क्या होता है देखिए.
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే copper wire, అదే battery — ఇది కేవలం పొడవుగా అయితే ఏమి జరుగుతుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: As the wire stretches from one metre to two, the current meter falls — all the way to half.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे wire एक मीटर से दो मीटर तक खिंचता है, current meter गिर जाता है — बिल्कुल आधे तक.
tts_sentences.text_te: wire ఒక మీటర్ నుండి రెండు మీటర్లకు సాగుతున్నప్పుడు, current meter పడిపోతుంది — సరిగ్గా సగానికి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Nothing about the copper changed. Length alone doubled the resistance R.
tts_sentences.text_hi: copper में कुछ भी नहीं बदला. सिर्फ length ने resistance R को दोगुना कर दिया.
tts_sentences.text_te: copper లో ఏమీ మారలేదు. length మాత్రమే resistance R ను రెట్టింపు చేసింది.

misconception_watch.belief: Resistance is fixed by the material; length and thickness don't matter.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The same copper wire, same fixed supply — stretched from 1 m to 2 m, the current meter falls to exactly half and the R readout doubles, live, on screen.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Same copper, twice the wire — twice the resistance R. Shape is half the story.

STATE_2.title: Wider frees: widen the same wire and the current climbs back

scene_composition.text: Now widen this same wire instead of stretching it.
As area A grows, more lanes open for the drifting electrons.
Resistance R has exactly halved — area and resistance move opposite ways.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now widen this same wire instead of stretching it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब इसी wire को खींचने के बजाय चौड़ा कीजिए.
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ఈ అదే wire ను సాగదీయకుండా వెడల్పు చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: As the cross-section area A grows, more lanes open for the drifting electrons, and the current climbs back up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे-जैसे cross-section area A बढ़ता है, drifting electrons के लिए और रास्ते खुलते हैं, और current फिर से बढ़ जाता है.
tts_sentences.text_te: cross-section area A పెరుగుతున్న కొద్దీ, drifting electrons కు మరిన్ని దారులు తెరుచుకుంటాయి, మరియు current మళ్ళీ పెరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Resistance R has exactly halved — area and resistance move opposite ways.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Resistance R बिल्कुल आधा हो गया है — area और resistance विपरीत दिशाओं में चलते हैं.
tts_sentences.text_te: Resistance R సరిగ్గా సగానికి తగ్గింది — area మరియు resistance వ్యతిరేక దిశల్లో కదులుతాయి.

STATE_3.title: PRIMARY aha — R = ρL/A: geometry dances, resistivity never moves

scene_composition.text: Watch the wire's shape dance — length stretches, then area widens, then both together.
The R readout swings through every change, but resistivity ρ never moves a single digit.
R equals ρL over A — shape is yours to engineer, ρ is the material's own fixed number.
Stretch the wire to double length at constant volume and R doesn't double — it quadruples.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the wire's shape dance — length stretches, then area widens, then both together.
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire के shape का बदलना देखिए — length खिंचती है, फिर area चौड़ा होता है, फिर दोनों साथ में.
tts_sentences.text_te: wire ఆకారం మారడం చూడండి — length సాగుతుంది, తర్వాత area వెడల్పు అవుతుంది, తర్వాత రెండూ కలిసి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The resistance R readout swings through every change, but resistivity rho never moves a single digit.
tts_sentences.text_hi: resistance R की reading हर बदलाव के साथ बदलती है, लेकिन resistivity rho एक अंक भी नहीं हिलता.
tts_sentences.text_te: resistance R రీడింగ్ ప్రతి మార్పుతో మారుతుంది, కానీ resistivity rho ఒక్క అంకె కూడా కదలదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: That's the law: R equals rho L over A — shape is yours to engineer, rho is the material's own fixed number.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही नियम है: R बराबर rho L over A — shape को आप engineer कर सकते हैं, rho material की अपनी fixed संख्या है.
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే నియమం: R అనేది rho L over A కు సమానం — shape ను మీరు engineer చేసుకోవచ్చు, rho అనేది material యొక్క సొంత స్థిర సంఖ్య.

misconception_watch.belief: R is a property of copper itself.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The R readout swinging through the full three-phase geometry cycle (including the ×4 constant-volume spike) while the ρ readout sits pinned at 1.700×10⁻⁸ Ω·m the entire time, both instruments visible simultaneously.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Rho belongs to the material; R = rho L over A belongs to this particular wire's shape.

STATE_4.title: What ρ is made of: swap the metal and ρ jumps sixty-five times

scene_composition.text: Keep the shape frozen and swap the metal itself — copper, then nichrome.
Nichrome's lattice ions collide with electrons far more often.
Resistivity rho jumps about sixty-five times higher.
Rho equals m over n e squared tau — a material's identity is its electron count n and collision time tau.

tts_sentences.text_en: Keep the shape frozen and swap the metal itself — copper, then nichrome.
tts_sentences.text_hi: shape को स्थिर रखते हुए metal को ही बदलिए — copper, फिर nichrome.
tts_sentences.text_te: shape ను స్థిరంగా ఉంచి, metal నే మార్చండి — copper, తర్వాత nichrome.

tts_sentences.text_en: Nichrome's lattice ions collide with electrons far more often, and resistivity rho jumps about sixty-five times higher.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Nichrome के lattice ions, electrons से कहीं ज्यादा बार टकराते हैं, और resistivity rho लगभग पैंसठ गुना ज्यादा बढ़ जाता है.
tts_sentences.text_te: Nichrome యొక్క lattice ions, electrons తో చాలా ఎక్కువగా ఢీకొంటాయి, మరియు resistivity rho దాదాపు అరవై ఐదు రెట్లు ఎక్కువగా పెరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Rho equals m over n e squared tau — a material's identity is its electron count n and its collision time tau, nothing geometric.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Rho बराबर m over n e squared tau — किसी material की पहचान उसका electron count n और collision time tau है, कोई geometric चीज़ नहीं.
tts_sentences.text_te: Rho అనేది m over n e squared tau కు సమానం — ఒక material యొక్క గుర్తింపు దాని electron count n మరియు collision time tau మాత్రమే, geometric ఏదీ కాదు.

STATE_5.title: Heat raises ρ: a hotter metal resists more, not less

scene_composition.text: Heat this copper wire from room temperature up toward two hundred twenty degrees.
The lattice ions shiver harder, collisions come sooner, and the current visibly slows.
Resistivity rho climbs by nearly eighty percent.
Rho at T equals rho-zero times one plus alpha delta T.

tts_sentences.text_en: Heat this copper wire from room temperature up toward two hundred twenty degrees.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस copper wire को room temperature से दो सौ बीस डिग्री तक गर्म कीजिए.
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ copper wire ను room temperature నుండి రెండు వందల ఇరవై డిగ్రీల వరకు వేడి చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The lattice ions shiver harder, collisions come sooner, and the current visibly slows — resistivity rho climbs by nearly eighty percent.
tts_sentences.text_hi: lattice ions और ज़ोर से कांपते हैं, collisions जल्दी होते हैं, और current साफ़ धीमा हो जाता है — resistivity rho लगभग अस्सी प्रतिशत बढ़ जाता है.
tts_sentences.text_te: lattice ions మరింత గట్టిగా కంపిస్తాయి, collisions త్వరగా జరుగుతాయి, మరియు current స్పష్టంగా నెమ్మదిస్తుంది — resistivity rho దాదాపు ఎనభై శాతం పెరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Heat shakes the lattice, relaxation time tau shrinks, and rho rises: rho at T equals rho-zero times one plus alpha delta T.
tts_sentences.text_hi: गर्मी lattice को हिलाती है, relaxation time tau घट जाता है, और rho बढ़ जाता है: rho at T बराबर rho-zero times one plus alpha delta T.
tts_sentences.text_te: వేడి lattice ను కదిలిస్తుంది, relaxation time tau తగ్గుతుంది, మరియు rho పెరుగుతుంది: rho at T అనేది rho-zero times one plus alpha delta T కు సమానం.

misconception_watch.visual_counter: Thermometer climbing → lattice visibly shivering harder → collision flashes coming sooner → drift slowing → current meter falling → rho readout climbing 1.78×, all at the same fixed supply.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Heat shakes the lattice, relaxation time tau shrinks — a hot metal resists MORE, not less.

STATE_7.title: Explore — all four dials yours: L, A, material, T

scene_composition.text: All four dials are live: L, A, material, T.
R and rho respond live off any dial.
Watch current, R, and rho all update together.

tts_sentences.text_en: All four dials are yours — length, area, material, temperature. Watch R and rho respond live.
tts_sentences.text_hi: चारों dials आपके हाथ में हैं — length, area, material, temperature. R और rho को live respond करते देखिए.
tts_sentences.text_te: నాలుగు dials మీ చేతుల్లోనే — length, area, material, temperature. R మరియు rho live గా ఎలా స్పందిస్తాయో చూడండి.

regeneration_variants.label: A water pipe network — pipe length and width matter, but so does what the pipe is made of
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A long, narrow pipe resists water flow more than a short, wide one — but a pipe lined with rough gravel resists more than a smooth one of the identical shape.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Same R = rho*L/A factorization — pipe length plays L, pipe width plays A, and the lining's roughness plays resistivity rho, the material's own fixed number independent of the pipe's shape.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Optional supplementary anchor; the primary teaching anchor stays the heater-coil / copper-cord pair.

regeneration_variants.label: Kitchen wiring — thick aluminium bus bars vs thin nichrome heating elements
regeneration_variants.physical_world: An electrician chooses thick, low-resistivity aluminium or copper for the wiring that carries current efficiently, but a thin, high-resistivity nichrome coil for a toaster or iron that is SUPPOSED to get hot.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Same R = rho*L/A engineering choice — same current electrical wiring, opposite intent: minimize R for the cord (low rho, thick, short), maximize R for the heating element (high rho, thin, long).
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Mirrors the primary heater-coil anchor from a household-electrician's decision-making angle.
